title: Define the 0.5.4 workflow-guidance response schema and lifecycle error contract
summary: Create the authoritative 0.5.4 contract for machine-readable lifecycle guidance so AI agents can determine canonical state transitions from `.rules` plus MCP tool responses without hidden assumptions. This ticket finalizes the normalized success and failure response schema for lifecycle-relevant tools, including canonical status/phase exposure, next-action guidance, terminality, follow-up obligations, active transaction identity, structured recovery/error semantics, stable error-code families, and backward-compatibility rules for legacy payload fields. Canonical history was repaired enough to unblock new work, and this ticket is now the active canonical task for defining the schema and implementation-ready contract notes that downstream 0.5.4 tickets will follow.
outputs: A documented normalized success-response schema for lifecycle-relevant MCP tools
A documented normalized failure-response schema for lifecycle and state-related failures
Explicit semantic definitions for `canonical_status`, `canonical_phase`, `next_action`, terminality, follow-up requirements, and active transaction identity
A stable error-family outline for begin/resume/terminal/integrity-related failures
A ticket-level contract foundation that later implementation tickets can apply consistently across tool surfaces
acceptance_criteria: The ticket defines a single authoritative response contract for lifecycle-aware success responses.
The ticket defines a single authoritative response contract for lifecycle-aware failure responses.
The contract explicitly distinguishes canonical status, canonical phase, next action, terminality, and follow-up obligations.
The contract explicitly defines active transaction identity fields needed for resume-vs-begin decisions.
The contract includes structured recovery guidance fields for begin-required, resume-required, terminal, and integrity-blocked failure classes.
The contract defines stable error-code families that later implementation tickets can apply consistently across lifecycle-aware tools.
The contract defines a backward-compatibility rule for retaining or omitting legacy/minimal payload fields.
The contract is specific enough that later implementation tickets can normalize tool responses without inventing per-tool semantics ad hoc.
The ticket does not weaken canonical lifecycle strictness or hide integrity drift.
tasks: Define the normalized success-response fields required for lifecycle-aware tools, including canonical state, next-action guidance, terminality, follow-up obligations, and active transaction identity.

Define the normalized failure-response fields required for lifecycle/state failures, including stable error codes, recovery guidance, recommended next tool/action, and integrity metadata when relevant.
Clarify semantic roles for status, phase, and next action so later implementation work can keep responses non-contradictory.
Document which fields are mandatory across all lifecycle-aware tools and which are context-dependent.
Resolve whether existing legacy/minimal fields remain alongside the new canonical response fields for compatibility.
Define stable error-code families for common lifecycle/state failures so downstream implementation work does not invent them piecemeal.
Leave implementation-ready notes for downstream tickets that will apply the schema to lifecycle wrappers, verify/commit helpers, and regression tests.
notes: This ticket is schema-definition work, not broad implementation work.
The ticket should preserve the existing strict canonical lifecycle model while making it safer for AI agents to consume.
The ticket should assume that machine-readable guidance is part of the public workflow contract, not just an implementation detail.
Canonical repair work cleared the previously blocking integrity drift enough to allow new canonical work to begin.
This ticket is now aligned with the active canonical task `p1-t01` and should be kept synchronized with canonical lifecycle progress as the schema definition advances.
The most important output of this ticket is an implementation-ready response contract that downstream tickets can apply consistently across lifecycle wrappers, helper tools, and regression coverage.
This ticket now includes the intended error-code families and the compatibility policy that should govern how legacy fields coexist with canonical workflow-guidance fields during rollout.

compatibility_policy.legacy_fields_policy: Preserve existing useful payload fields where practical during the 0.5.4 transition, but require canonical workflow guidance fields to be present for lifecycle-aware responses.
compatibility_policy.success_policy: Legacy fields such as `event` and `payload` may remain, but must not be the only lifecycle decision surface.
compatibility_policy.failure_policy: Human-readable exception text may remain, but structured failure fields become the authoritative recovery contract.
compatibility_policy.omission_rule: Contextual fields may be omitted only when truly unknown or not applicable, not merely because the tool currently returns a minimal payload.

semantic_constraints: `committed` must remain non-terminal until explicit lifecycle end.
`next_action` must remain the authoritative field for the next lifecycle step.
Helper success must not imply terminal completion unless canonical state is actually terminal.
Structured success responses must describe resulting canonical state after the operation completes, not a guessed pre-operation snapshot.
Structured failure responses must distinguish begin-required, resume-required, terminal, and integrity-blocked cases without relying only on prose.

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Keep this ticket synchronized with active canonical lifecycle progress while schema-definition work is in progress.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: active canonical task started successfully and schema-definition work is now unblocked
